defectIssueTypesRationale: Includes both 'Defect' (standard bug classification) and 'Ticket' (support agents sometimes create customer-reported bugs with this type instead of Defect). Both are captured to avoid undercounting. Reviewed 2026-06-29 against a secondary dashboard — 3 real GenAI Lens bugs were missed because they were created as Ticket type (TRITON-36645, TRITON-37086, TRITON-37110).

severityWeights.comment: Each escaped Defect contributes its priority weight to a severity-weighted count (Rec #3). Higher severity counts more, continuously — replaces the old crude floor. Unlisted priorities default to 1.

tritonCurve.comment: 0-10 TRITON score = scaleMax * CDF((weightedCount - mu)/sigma), CDF per modelParams.curveType. mu is the SLA ANCHOR: severity-weighted escapes/quarter treated as 'medium' (5/10) — a target, not the observed mean (Rec #2,#5). Team-confirmed mu=5 for Q3 2026.

modelParams.comment: UI scoring knobs emitted for the page (Rec #5,#7). curveType: gaussian|logistic (#5). baseFloor: min Base-Risk value on 0-10 at the lowest qualitative level. TEAM-CONFIRMED baseFloor=0 for Q3 2026 (mapping 1->0, max discrimination); 1.5-3.33 would keep a floor so a core product is never 0 inherent risk (#7). multiplierMin/Max: mitigation multiplier at strongest/weakest. Team chose SYMMETRIC 0.80/1.20 for Q3 (weak mitigation penalized as much as strong is rewarded, ±20%); the original asymmetric was 0.70/1.10.

calibration.comment: Recalibration cadence (Rec #5). mu/sigma are anchored, reviewed on this cadence — NOT per quarter — to keep quarter-over-quarter scores comparable. Recompute basis from accumulated history once >=4 quarters exist.
calibration.basis: Q2-2026 observed distribution + team SLA target

exposure.comment: Exposure normalization (Rec #1) -> DEFECT DENSITY = weightedCount / exposure * referenceExposure. OFF. FINDING (verified 2026-06 in Jira): cross-team density is NOT viable with current data. 'releases' unusable (fixVersions not tied to issues). 'delivered' denominators are wildly inconsistent across teams — even clean User-Story counts this quarter: GAIL 67, HZN 62, MIRA 12, MOB 378 (31x spread reflecting ticketing style, not real output). Normalizing cross-team would peg Mira at max and Mobile near zero. VIABLE PATH instead: per-project trend (each project vs its OWN history), which needs accumulated quarters (ties to #5/#8). Keep disabled until then.

methodology: Confluence QA Risk Baseline 2026 §4.6 — Defects and Tickets by component, service requests excluded by summary keyword, Mira/Mira Studio split by linked issue (HZN-* -> Mira Studio; MIRA-*/MIRALEGACY-* -> Mira). Issue types: Defect + Ticket (see defectIssueTypesRationale). Classification field: TRITON 'component' (standard Jira field, reliably filled by support) — NOT 'dev feature[dropdown]' (custom field, inconsistently filled; verified 2026-06-29: 4 valid GenAI Lens Defects had component set but no dev feature value, and would be silently missed by the alternative filter).
comment: Maps each Risk Baseline project to its TRITON component, per Confluence 'QA Risk Baseline 2026' §4.6. excludeKeywords filter out service requests by summary (reviewed start of each quarter — use specific phrases, not single words, to avoid false positives on real bugs). Mira and Mira Studio share the TRITON component 'Mira'; linkAttribution splits them by linked issue project key prefix. MIRALEGACY is counted as Mira (team decision, not in §4.6).
qaPreProductionReportersRationale: Bugs labeled 'known' and reported by QA team members on dev boards — found in staging before reaching production. Strongest positive signal for QA effectiveness (3-tier quality hierarchy: customer-reported < QA-found-in-production < QA-found-in-staging). Reporter list reviewed 2026-06-29. Account ID (557058:...) included alongside emails for Jira compatibility.
